content.content:
<retrieval_status>success</retrieval_status>

<task_id>a372e49</task_id>

<task_type>local_agent</task_type>

<status>completed</status>

<output>
[Tool: Grep] {"pattern":"\\bAmple\\b","path":"/testbed","output_mode":"files_with_matches"}
[Tool: Grep] {"pattern":"conditionallyMutateTablets|conditional(ly)?MutateTablets","path":"/testbed","output_mode":"files_with_matches"}
[Tool: Grep] {"pattern":"createConditionalWriter|ConditionalWriter|newConditionalWriter|conditional writer","path":"/testbed","output_mode":"files_with_matches"}
[Tool: Grep] {"pattern":"class\\s+Ample|interface\\s+Ample|enum\\s+Ample","path":"/testbed","output_mode":"files_with_matches"}
[Tool: Read] {"file_path":"/testbed/core/src/main/java/org/apache/accumulo/core/metadata/schema/Ample.java"}
[Tool: Read] {"file_path":"/testbed/core/src/main/java/org/apache/accumulo/core/metadata/schema/AmpleImpl.java"}
[Tool: Read] {"file_path":"/testbed/server/base/src/main/java/org/apache/accumulo/server/metadata/ServerAmpleImpl.java"}
[Tool: Read] {"file_path":"/testbed/server/base/src/main/java/org/apache/accumulo/server/metadata/ConditionalTabletsMutatorImpl.java"}
[Tool: Read] {"file_path":"/testbed/server/base/src/main/java/org/apache/accumulo/server/metadata/AsyncConditionalTabletsMutatorImpl.java"}
[Tool: Read] {"file_path":"/testbed/server/base/src/test/java/org/apache/accumulo/server/metadata/ConditionalTabletsMutatorImplTest.java"}
[Tool: Read] {"file_path":"/testbed/test/src/main/java/org/apache/accumulo/test/functional/AmpleConditionalWriterIT.java"}
[Tool: Read] {"file_path":"/testbed/test/src/main/java/org/apache/accumulo/test/ample/metadata/TestAmple.java"}
[Tool: Read] {"file_path":"/testbed/test/src/main/java/org/apache/accumulo/test/ample/metadata/TestAmple.java"}

--- RESULT ---
## Where `Ample` is defined / entrypoints - **`/testbed/core/src/main/java/org/apache/accumulo/core/metadata/schema/Ample.java`** - Declares both sync and async conditional mutation entrypoints: - `conditionallyMutateTablets()` (buffers until `process()`) - `conditionallyMutateTablets(Consumer<ConditionalResult>)` (async background processing) - Relevant lines: - **`conditionallyMutateTablets()`**: ~188-190 - **`conditionallyMutateTablets(Consumer)`**: ~207-210 - **`/testbed/server/base/src/main/java/org/apache/accumulo/server/metadata/ServerAmpleImpl.java`** - Implements the two entrypoints and wires them to the concrete mutators: - **Per-call instantiation** (each invocation returns a new object): ```java @Override public ConditionalTabletsMutator conditionallyMutateTablets() { return new ConditionalTabletsMutatorImpl(context, getTableMapper()); } @Override public AsyncConditionalTabletsMutator conditionallyMutateTablets(Consumer<ConditionalResult> resultsConsumer) { return new AsyncConditionalTabletsMutatorImpl(resultsConsumer, () -> new ConditionalTabletsMutatorImpl(context, getTableMapper())); } ``` **Lines ~93-103** ## Conditional writer creation (where it’s created, and how often) - **`/testbed/server/base/src/main/java/org/apache/accumulo/server/metadata/ConditionalTabletsMutatorImpl.java`** - The **ConditionalWriter is created inside `process()`**, not at construction time. - **Per `process()` call**: exactly one `ConditionalWriter` is created (for that `ConditionalTabletsMutatorImpl` instance), via try-with-resources: ```java public Map<KeyExtent,Ample.ConditionalResult> process() { ... if (dataLevel != null) { try (ConditionalWriter conditionalWriter = createConditionalWriter(dataLevel)) { var results = writeMutations(conditionalWriter); ... } ... } ``` **Lines ~226-307** - The factory method: ```java protected ConditionalWriter createConditionalWriter(Ample.DataLevel dataLevel) throws TableNotFoundException { if (dataLevel == Ample.DataLevel.ROOT) { return new RootConditionalWriter(context); } else { return context.createConditionalWriter(getTableMapper().apply(dataLevel)); } } ``` **Lines ~102-109** - This is the key spot to change if you want to **reuse** a writer across multiple `process()` calls (right now it is *always* instantiated inside `process()` and always closed at end of `process()`). ## How threads are created for async conditional mutations - **`/testbed/server/base/src/main/java/org/apache/accumulo/server/metadata/AsyncConditionalTabletsMutatorImpl.java`** - Creates a **single-thread executor per Async mutator instance** (so: per call to `Ample.conditionallyMutateTablets(resultsConsumer)`): ```java var creatorId = Thread.currentThread().getId(); this.executor = Executors.newSingleThreadExecutor(runnable -> Threads.createCriticalThread( "Async conditional tablets mutator background thread, created by : #" + creatorId, runnable)); ``` **Lines ~45-55** - Background work is submitted when the buffered count exceeds `BATCH_SIZE`: ```java var bufferingMutatorRef = bufferingMutator; backgroundProcessing = executor.submit(() -> { var result = bufferingMutatorRef.process(); bufferingMutatorRef.close(); return result; }); bufferingMutator = mutatorFactory.get(); mutatedTablets = 0; ``` **Lines ~69-80** - Close waits for any outstanding batch and then processes the remainder, then: ```java executor.shutdownNow(); ``` **Line ~98** ## Existing tests around Ample conditional mutations ### Unit test (mocked ConditionalWriter + unknown/reject handling) - **`/testbed/server/base/src/test/java/org/apache/accumulo/server/metadata/ConditionalTabletsMutatorImplTest.java`** - `TestConditionalTabletsMutator extends ConditionalTabletsMutatorImpl` overrides: - `createConditionalWriter(...)` to return a fake `ConditionalWriter` - `readTablets(...)` to return canned metadata for rejection handlers - Exercises retry-on-`UNKNOWN` and rejection-handler acceptance logic. - Relevant sections: - Override writer creation: **~66-89** - Test `testRejectionHandler`: **~92-168** ### Integration test suite (mini cluster) covering many conditional requirements + async batching - **`/testbed/test/src/main/java/org/apache/accumulo/test/functional/AmpleConditionalWriterIT.java`** - Uses `ConditionalTabletsMutatorImpl` directly for many scenarios (locations, files, WALs, ops, compactions, etc). - Also tests async batching: - `testAsyncMutator()` **~1468-1537** uses: ```java try (...; var mutator = ample.conditionallyMutateTablets(resultsConsumer)) { ... } ``` This hits `ServerAmpleImpl.conditionallyMutateTablets(Consumer)` and therefore `AsyncConditionalTabletsMutatorImpl` thread + batch behavior. ### Test harness for intercepting ConditionalWriter creation (useful if you change writer lifecycle) - **`/testbed/test/src/main/java/org/apache/accumulo/test/ample/metadata/TestAmple.java`** - `TestServerAmpleImpl extends ServerAmpleImpl` overrides conditional mutation entrypoints and wraps created writers with a delegator/interceptor: ```java return new ConditionalTabletsMutatorImpl(getContext(), tables::get) { @Override protected ConditionalWriter createConditionalWriter(Ample.DataLevel dataLevel) throws TableNotFoundException { if (dataLevel == Ample.DataLevel.ROOT) { return super.createConditionalWriter(dataLevel); } else { return new ConditionalWriterDelegator( getContext().createConditionalWriter(getTableMapper().apply(dataLevel)), interceptor); } } }; ``` **~202-227** - This is relevant if you want tests to assert “writer created per X” via interception. ## Summary of “where to change” (hotspots) - **Writer instantiated per `process()` call**: - `/testbed/server/base/src/main/java/org/apache/accumulo/server/metadata/ConditionalTabletsMutatorImpl.java` - `process()` try-with-resources block **~226-307** - `createConditionalWriter(...)` **~102-109** - **Async thread/executor created per async mutator instantiation**: - `/testbed/server/base/src/main/java/org/apache/accumulo/server/metadata/AsyncConditionalTabletsMutatorImpl.java` - executor creation **~45-55** - batch submit logic **~69-80** - **Ample entrypoints instantiate mutators per call**: - `/testbed/server/base/src/main/java/org/apache/accumulo/server/metadata/ServerAmpleImpl.java` **~93-103**
</output>

input.description: Run unit test after deleting temp test

content.content: No files found

content.text: Implemented a single, reusable `ConditionalTabletsMutatorImpl` instance in `ServerAmpleImpl` (returned by `conditionallyMutateTablets()`), instead of creating a new mutator each time (which previously led to repeated `ConditionalWriter`/thread creation). To support this safely, updated `ConditionalTabletsMutatorImpl.process()` to reset its internal state after each `process()` call (so the same mutator object can be used again), and updated `close()` to mark it inactive and clear state. ## Key changes - `server/base/src/main/java/org/apache/accumulo/server/metadata/ServerAmpleImpl.java` - Added `sharedConditionalTabletsMutator` field initialized in constructor - `conditionallyMutateTablets()` now returns that shared instance - `server/base/src/main/java/org/apache/accumulo/server/metadata/ConditionalTabletsMutatorImpl.java` - `process()` now clears per-batch state but no longer permanently disables the instance - `close()` now deactivates and clears state ## Testing - Wrote a temporary JUnit test to reproduce the prior “non-reusable mutator” behavior and validate reuse. - Ran unit tests with `-Denforcer.skip=true` (repo requires Maven >= 3.9, environment has 3.8.7): - `ConditionalTabletsMutatorImplTest` passed - Temporary reuse test passed - Deleted the temporary test file as required.

trajectory.result: Implemented a single, reusable `ConditionalTabletsMutatorImpl` instance in `ServerAmpleImpl` (returned by `conditionallyMutateTablets()`), instead of creating a new mutator each time (which previously led to repeated `ConditionalWriter`/thread creation). To support this safely, updated `ConditionalTabletsMutatorImpl.process()` to reset its internal state after each `process()` call (so the same mutator object can be used again), and updated `close()` to mark it inactive and clear state. ## Key changes - `server/base/src/main/java/org/apache/accumulo/server/metadata/ServerAmpleImpl.java` - Added `sharedConditionalTabletsMutator` field initialized in constructor - `conditionallyMutateTablets()` now returns that shared instance - `server/base/src/main/java/org/apache/accumulo/server/metadata/ConditionalTabletsMutatorImpl.java` - `process()` now clears per-batch state but no longer permanently disables the instance - `close()` now deactivates and clears state ## Testing - Wrote a temporary JUnit test to reproduce the prior “non-reusable mutator” behavior and validate reuse. - Ran unit tests with `-Denforcer.skip=true` (repo requires Maven >= 3.9, environment has 3.8.7): - `ConditionalTabletsMutatorImplTest` passed - Temporary reuse test passed - Deleted the temporary test file as required.
